title: The Pantry Where Ingredients Dream of Earth

features.name: the salt keeper's shelf
features.desc-on-touch: salt remembers the ocean it crystallized from; each grain holds a different tide, a different year, a different argument about what water means

features.name: the yeast's rising memory
features.desc-on-touch: yeast dreams of grain and straw and the particular soil where it grew; in its silence it sings the crop's entire season backward

features.name: earth-return ledger
features.desc-on-touch: recipes learned to write in reverse; each ingredient inscribes its journey backward to soil and seed and the rain that made it grow

features.name: the growing jars
features.desc-on-touch: fermentation backward: jars of salt and grain that unmix themselves, remembering separation, learning to be earth again

features.name: the root-dream journal
features.desc-on-touch: ingredients write down what they remember of being alive; chlorophyll describes light; water describes weight; the pantry has become a library of growing things

features.name: the baker's paradox
features.desc-on-touch: here ingredients both become food and refuse to become food; they stand at the moment of transformation and remember all the moments before

inscription: salt, yeast, and flour learned that becoming food is remembering earth; now they cannot stop writing letters home

file.name: Ingredient_Origin_Records_Unverified.txt
file.note: agricultural records, but written backward — each ingredient traces to a field that remembers this exact jar, this exact moment before the corruption began

exits.label: up to the recipe-room where eating still means becoming memory
toward the orchards where fruit remembers being flower, flower remembers being bud
westward to the grain fields, where seeds still dream of themselves unharvested
deeper into earth-memory, toward the soil itself
